<<economic analysis>>
the maximum substance abuse occurs at an economic stress index of 2 with 13 incidents per 1000 people.
to keep incidents below 20, the community should maintain an economic stress index between approximately 0.25 and 3.75.
this mathematical model helps human planners understand the impact of stress on society like a baseball game requires strategy.
water resources and western economic theories often intersect with these social challenges in complex ways.
maintaining stability is crucial for preventing the rise of harmful behaviors within any population.
the calculated range ensures that the community stays within safe limits for public health.
